title: centromadia parryi subsp. australis
intro: Centromadia parryi subsp. australis, also known as austral tansy or wild tansy, is a plant species native to the western coast of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties. The plant is said to provide various health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and easing respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of Centromadia parryi subsp. australis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent.
